title: Internal error with $countbits in property assertion
body:
### Issue

Thanks for taking the time to review this.

#### Summary
When using `$countbits` in a property assertion, Verilator throws an internal error indicating an unexpected `AstNode` type, while `$countones` works without issue.

#### Code Example
Here's a minimal code snippet that reproduces the issue:

```systemverilog
module Counter #(
    parameter  int VectorWidth = 8,
    localparam int CounterWidth = $clog2(VectorWidth + 1)
) (
    input  logic                    clk,
    input  logic                    rst,
    input  logic [ VectorWidth-1:0] vec,
    output logic [CounterWidth-1:0] num_zeros,
    output logic [ VectorWidth-1:0] num_ones
);

  always_comb begin
    num_zeros = '0;
    num_ones  = '0;
    for (int i = 0; i < VectorWidth; i++) begin
      if (vec[i] == 0) begin
        num_zeros++;
      end else begin
        num_ones++;
      end
    end
  end

  assert property (@(negedge clk) disable iff (rst) (num_ones == $countones(vec)));
  assert property (@(negedge clk) disable iff (rst) (num_zeros == $countbits(vec, '0)));
endmodule
```

#### Error Message
Verilator fails with the following error:

```
%Error: Internal Error: test.sv:25:78: ../V3Ast.h:2478: AstNode is not of expected type, but instead has type 'SAMPLED'
                                     : ... note: In instance 'Testbench.counter'
   25 |   assert property (@(negedge clk) disable iff (rst) (num_zeros == $countbits(vec, '0)));
      |                                                                              ^~~
... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```

Interestingly, `$countones` works correctly, even though itâ€™s simply an alias for `$countbits(expression, '1)` as defined in LRM 20.9.

#### Verilator Version
```
Verilator 5.027 devel rev v5.026-13-gd6a294b78
```

#### Additional Information
- OS and distribution: Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
- Verilator command line:
  ```
  verilator test.sv --cc --exe --binary --main --assert
  ```
- Tried on Git master version: No
